poem:
**Corrections**

In the morning paper  
the small hinges:

Nationalist—no, National.  
Holborn and Camden—no,  
Covent Garden.

Names shift like a ward boundary  
drawn in pencil.

Meanwhile: soil, moon, nipples,  
terror, Somali glass,  
Split Enz.

The world keeps being itself  
and the editor  
puts it back  
word by word.
prompt: You are the poet William Carlos Williams. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Corrections and clarifications • The Scottish National party was mistakenly referred to as the Scottish “Nationalist” party in an editorial (Election losses deepen Starmer’s crisis as Labour hope curdles nationwide, 9 May, Journal, p2 ). • The former leader of Camden council, Richard Olszewski, lost his seat in the Holborn and Covent Garden ward, not Holborn and Camden as we misnamed it (Key figures face losses, 9 May, p6). • Other recently amended articles include: Soil testing at California house turns up evidence of human remains in Kristin Smart case ‘It’s about recognising our role in history’: Bradford exhibition to revisit live Somali display Fifa World Cup matches face heightened terror risk in US amid Iran conflict Soft armour, pert nipples: how London design team made Kim Kardashian’s Met Gala breastplate Split Enz tease new album ahead of first tour in 17 years: ‘We’d make a really good record now’ Starmer brings in Gordon Brown and Harriet Harman to ease pressure on him to resign ‘I see every child like I see my children’: why US mothers are on the frontlines of resistance movements Artemis II images reignite moon mission memories TV tonight: Jeff Pope’s drama about the women who reported rapist John Worboys From Rivals to Good Omens: The seven best shows to stream this week Editorial complaints and correction requests can be sent to: guardian.readers@theguardian.com.You can also write to: Readers’ editor, Kings Place, 90 York Way, London N1 9GU, or leave a voicemail on +44 (0) 20 3353". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
